name: Tamashiro Market
tagline: Honolulu's most trusted fish market for over 80 years — where local families buy poke and fresh seafood for parties.
parking: Small lot in front; street parking on N King St
talkStory: Open since the 1940s, Tamashiro Market is a Kalihi institution. The poke counter has a dozen varieties daily — shoyu ahi, spicy tako, limu, and seasonal specials. Come Saturday morning before 10am for the best selection; by noon, the popular cuts are gone. Locals grab a styrofoam plate and eat standing in the parking lot. The fish counter also sells whole fish, sashimi-grade blocks, and shellfish at prices well below supermarkets.
